findings.summary: Advanced-mode inference omits documented phase-config fallback, creating contradictory behavior.
evidence: The comment says phase config should imply advanced mode for backward compatibility.
The implementation returns only `advancedMode === true || motionMode === 'advanced' || motionMode === 'presets'` and never checks `phaseConfig`.
findings.suggestion: Add an explicit phase-config fallback in the condition (for legacy payloads) or remove the contradictory backward-compat comment and enforce strict mode flags.

findings.summary: Inline edit passes a no-op add-to-references handler, producing an interactive control with no effect.
evidence: `InlineEditCanvas` passes `handleAddToReferences={async () => {}}`.
The same call hard-codes `isAddingToReferences={false}` and `addToReferencesSuccess={false}`, so the button state never changes and the click path does nothing.
findings.suggestion: Provide a real add-to-references implementation in inline edit, or explicitly hide/disable that control in this mode to avoid a dead interaction path.

findings.summary: Before-unload flush captures stale project/query context due incomplete effect dependencies.
evidence: `flushPendingSettings` uses outer `projectId` and `queryClient` values.

The `beforeunload` effect depends on `[toolId, scope, isCustomMode, customSaveRef]` and does not include `projectId`, `queryClient`, or `flushPendingSettings`, so the handler can run with outdated context after state changes.
findings.suggestion: Memoize `flushPendingSettings` with `useCallback` and include it in effect dependencies (or include `projectId`/`queryClient` directly) so unload flush logic always uses current context.

findings.summary: Pair-prompt targets are computed but never used, leaving dead logic in task apply flow.
evidence: `applySettingsFromTask` builds `pairPromptTargets` from `preparePairPromptTargets(pairPromptSnapshot)` and then never references `pairPromptTargets`.
The code still performs snapshot preparation work before continuing to unrelated apply steps (`applyModelSettings`, `applyPromptSettings`, etc.).
findings.suggestion: Either remove the unused snapshot-target preparation path or wire `pairPromptTargets` into the prompt-application step so the branch has an effect.

findings.summary: Overloaded `context`/`ctx`/`applyContext` names obscure different data shapes
evidence: The hook accepts `context: ApplySettingsContext`, repeatedly aliases it to `ctx`, and then derives another object called `applyContext`.
These similarly named values represent different contracts (hook-side state vs service apply payload) but are passed through the same call chain.
findings.suggestion: Use distinct intent-revealing names (for example `handlerState`, `latestHandlerState`, `serviceApplyContext`) and align helper signatures with those names.

findings.summary: `asRecord` has different fallback semantics across modules
evidence: `asRecord` in `payloadBuilder.ts` returns `{}` for non-object inputs.
`asRecord` in `individualTravelSegment.ts` also returns `{}` for non-object inputs.
`asRecord` in `VideoTravelDetails.tsx` returns `undefined` for non-object inputs.
findings.suggestion: Rename by behavior (`toRecordOrEmpty` vs `toRecordOrUndefined`) or unify a single shared helper contract so nullability is predictable from the name.

findings.summary: Feature modules named `helpers`/`utils` hide domain intent at import sites
evidence: `platformInstall/helpers.ts` contains core domain logic like `detectPlatform`, `detectBrowser`, and `getInstallMethod`.
`sourceImageChanges/helpers.ts` contains mismatch-analysis workflow (`buildMismatchMap`) rather than generic utility snippets.
Multiple feature folders expose similarly named `utils` modules, reducing import-time clarity compared with domain-specific filenames.
findings.suggestion: Rename these files to domain-specific module names (for example `platformDetection.ts`, `sourceMismatchMap.ts`, `variantPresentation.ts`, `promptSubmissionTransforms.ts`) and keep temporary re-export shims during migration.

findings.summary: Image-to-image code uses conflicting terms (`I2I` vs `img2img`) and field styles
evidence: `src/shared/lib/tasks/zImageTurboI2I.ts` uses symbols like `ZImageTurboI2ITaskParams` and `createBatchZImageTurboI2ITasks`.
`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` and `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts` use `Img2Img`/`img2img*` names for the same capability.
`zImageTurboI2I.ts` also mixes `numImages` (batch input) and `num_images` (single-task input) for the same concept.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one internal TS term (`imageToImage` or `img2img`) and one TS field style (`numImages`), then map to backend literals (`z_image_turbo_i2i`, `num_images`) only at payload boundaries.

findings.summary: `types/shots.ts` name no longer matches what the barrel exports
evidence: `src/types/shots.ts` exports `GenerationParams`, `GenerationMetadata`, and `GenerationRow` alongside `Shot` types.
The file header describes it as a shot/generation compatibility barrel, but the module path still implies shot-only contents.
findings.suggestion: Introduce an intent-revealing barrel for generation + shot types (or split into explicit generation and shot barrels), migrate imports, and keep `src/types/shots.ts` as a temporary deprecated re-export.

findings.summary: Image edit state builder relies on casts that bypass mode/shape checks.
evidence: `UseImageEditValueParams` declares `editMode` as `string | null` instead of the `ImageEditState['editMode']` union (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts:18`).
`editMode` is force-cast into the target type (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts:128`).
The final return object is cast wholesale with `as ImageEditState`, which suppresses compile-time detection of missing/incorrect fields (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts:225`).
findings.suggestion: Tighten `UseImageEditValueParams` to exact `ImageEditState` field types and replace the final assertion with `satisfies ImageEditState` (or explicit typed object construction) so drift is caught by the compiler.

findings.summary: Shared task query key caches incompatible raw/camelCase shapes.
evidence: `preloadGenerationTaskMappings` prefetches `taskQueryKeys.single(taskId)` and returns raw Supabase row data (`select('*')`) without mapping (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts:129-140`).
`enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData` reads the same key as `Task` via `getQueryData<Task>(...)` (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts:176`).
`useGetTask` for the same key maps DB rows through `mapDbTaskToTask` (camelCase `Task` shape), creating a second, incompatible contract (`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:297-312`).
findings.suggestion: Use one canonical cache shape for `taskQueryKeys.single`: either map bridge-prefetched rows through `mapDbTaskToTask` before caching, or use a separate key for raw task rows.

findings.summary: Tooltip children/ref typings are broader than runtime single-element contract.
evidence: `TouchableTooltipProps` allows `children: ReactNode`, but implementation requires `React.Children.only(children)` and casts to `ReactElement` (`src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx:55,95`).
`TooltipTrigger` ref is typed as `HTMLButtonElement` even when `asChild` can render other element types through `render` (`src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx:13-19`).
findings.suggestion: Type `children` as `ReactElement` for paths that require a single element, and use `React.ElementRef<typeof TooltipPrimitive.Trigger>` (or a discriminated `asChild` prop type) so ref/child types match runtime behavior.

findings.summary: Trimmed-duration label can call toFixed on non-number params.
evidence: `trimmed_duration` is force-cast from JSON-like params to `number | undefined` (`src/shared/components/VariantSelector/utils.ts:24`).
The next line calls `duration.toFixed(1)` after only a truthy check (`src/shared/components/VariantSelector/utils.ts:25`), so string/object values can bypass typing and fail at runtime.
findings.suggestion: Parse with a runtime guard before formatting, e.g. read the raw value, coerce only if numeric, then call `toFixed` only when `Number.isFinite(duration)` is true.
